title: Maze Graphs, Connectivity, Cycles, Components, and Trees · Code Recall
summary: A delayed puzzle review reconstructs maze graphs, connectivity, cycles, components, and trees from revision, configuration, seed, topology, frontier, predecessor map, state transition, draw record, accessibility report, profile, artifact, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for validated maze graph adapter after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map each cell to a vertex and each reciprocal open passage to an undirected edge without leaking render state.
Use traversal evidence to count connected components and produce a witness for an unreachable cell.
Detect or construct a cycle witness rather than infer cycles from edge count alone on an unvalidated graph.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State vertices, edges, representation, traversal work, memory, and grid-specific bounds.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Graph Complexity, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Graph Complexity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Graph Complexity, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A review puzzle team handles case 4097 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate graph mapping, connectivity, components, cycles, and tree claims; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: State vertices, edges, representation, traversal work, memory, and grid-specific bounds.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Graph Complexity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Algorithm complexity can be reported without defining graph representation.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Graph Mapping to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Graph Mapping.

checks.description: The decision applies Connectivity to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Connectivity.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Cycle Evidence.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Tree Invariants.

checks.description: The decision applies Graph Complexity to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Graph Complexity.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Graph Complexity.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using state and vertices plus specific case evidence.
